steps.title: Build the indirect pathway
steps.content: Vega and Price propose that a "news finds me" attitude may reduce voting probability through an indirect effect. Support needs two links: NFM weakens political knowledge or interest, and weaker knowledge or interest lowers the likelihood of voting. Without both links, the finding would not explain how passive news acquisition could affect voting indirectly.

steps.title: Reject broken links
steps.content: A discusses election timing rather than the knowledge-and-interest pathway. C says NFM has little relation to the intermediate variables, undermining the pathway. D gives only a conditional and incomplete link through social network size. The remaining finding says NFM reduces knowledge and interest and that those traits are positively tied to voting, establishing the whole chain. The answer is B.
